description_long: This is a large, bulbous ceramic vase with a single, thick handle connecting the shoulder to the neck. The neck is short and widens into a broad, flat rim, which appears slightly uneven. The body of the vase is decorated with faint, incised geometric patterns, primarily consisting of cross-hatched lines. The surface is matte and rough, with a pale, off-white coloration and some areas showing subtle reddish-brown undertones. A significant portion of the lower body is broken away, exposing the hollow interior and giving the vase a visibly damaged appearance. The overall form suggests it was once a utilitarian vessel, possibly for water or storage, now aged and weathered.
description: A large, pale ceramic vase with a single handle, geometric incised patterns, and a wide, flat rim. The lower body is heavily broken, revealing the interior and giving it a weathered, ancient look.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from the side, showing the handle and the incised geometric patterns on the body, with the rim and neck clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the extensive breakage at the base, exposing the hollow interior and the thickness of the ceramic walls.
description_view_2: The broken section is prominent, with the handle partially visible and the rim seen at an angle, emphasizing the damage.
description_view_3: The back of the vase is shown, with the handle fully visible and the surface appearing smoother, with less visible patterning and breakage.
